briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon. I will now begin the daily briefing by the Climate Change Spokesperson on May 7, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Deepening Climate Crisis and Warnings from Home and Abroad
sections.content: The news released today demonstrates that the impact of the climate crisis on our lives is intensifying across all fronts. Emperor penguins have been designated as an endangered species 124 years after their discovery, with a shocking projection that their population will halve by 2073 due to climate change [7]. Internationally, the possibility of tsunamis hundreds of meters high and the triggering of mega-tsunami waves in Alaska have been mentioned [9, 131], and news of a second large-scale tsunami being triggered in two years has also been reported [130]. Furthermore, hidden methane is being emitted from beneath Greenland's ice [123], and research indicates that climate change is reducing the nutritional value of food, while the Great Pacific Garbage Patch is generating microplastics, contributing to climate change [124, 126]. Voices emphasizing the urgency of climate change response are growing louder, with high-temperature damage being recognized as a constant disaster both domestically and internationally, and Gyeonggi Provincial Assembly members urging practical support for farmers and fishermen affected by climate change [5, 6, 8]. Notably, an emergency debate was held in the National Assembly, stating that amendments to the Carbon Neutrality Framework Act can no longer be delayed, signaling the need for institutional improvements [29].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Acceleration of Domestic Local Governments' Efforts to Achieve Carbon Neutrality
sections.content: Amidst this crisis, domestic local governments are steadily continuing their efforts to achieve carbon neutrality. Suwon City is investing 50 billion won to fully launch a 'Carbon Neutral Green City' centered in Gosaek-dong, recognizing it as a driver of urban competitiveness and accelerating its development [10, 13, 22, 23, 24, 33, 51, 63, 66, 75]. Gwangmyeong City is expanding green building initiatives beyond the public sector to the private sector, accelerating the realization of a carbon-neutral, energy-independent city [15, 16, 17, 28, 32, 36, 40, 48, 52, 54, 55, 59, 74, 82]. The Happy City Agency (Haengbokcheong) has launched an expert advisory group and is promoting the expansion of green infrastructure, strengthening its cooperation system to achieve its 2040 carbon neutrality city goal [18, 19, 20, 31, 49, 58, 76]. Gangjin County is accelerating green space expansion by creating carbon-neutral urban forests at intersections [21, 62, 72, 77], and Chungcheongnam-do is spreading its 'Carbon Neutral Volunteer' model nationwide, encouraging practical action even in rural areas [14, 39, 47, 61, 73, 86]. Furthermore, Seongbuk-gu and Gimhae City are operating village-type environmental education programs centered around the 'Seongbuk Carbon Neutral Learning Center' [25, 37, 42, 53, 84, 85], and promoting carbon neutrality education and leadership training programs for youth, striving to raise citizen awareness [26, 38, 45, 65, 70, 71, 80, 89].

sections.title: 🌱 Trends in Climate Change Adaptation and Carbon Reduction in Agriculture and Industry
sections.content: Active efforts for climate change adaptation and carbon reduction are also underway in specific industrial and agricultural sectors. The Climate Change Center and Seoul National University Climate Tech Center are discussing ways to apply methane management systems to industries, exploring the introduction of carbon reduction technologies in the industrial sector [1]. In agriculture, Gimpo City's automated seedling farm is gaining attention for its role as a safeguard for agriculture amidst climate change [2], and Hongcheon County is piloting the distribution of 'sunshade nets' to apple farms to combat high-temperature damage [3, 11]. Next year's livestock budget is also planned with an emphasis on 'productivity' and 'carbon neutrality' [30], indicating an expansion of carbon reduction efforts in the livestock industry. In addition, an article has been published suggesting that carbon neutrality practices can begin in a single rice paddy field [87], demonstrating that concrete action plans for climate change response are being discussed across various sectors.

sections.title: ⚡️ Domestic and International Status and Challenges of Renewable Energy Transition
sections.content: The transition to renewable energy is a core pillar of climate change response, but challenges still remain. Gunsan City is conducting a demand survey for a 5 billion won renewable energy convergence support project by 2027, aiming to establish an energy self-sufficiency base [91, 93, 96, 97, 101, 102, 103, 110], and Korea Electric Power Corporation (KEPCO) is contributing to the expansion of renewable energy supply by constructing 95MW solar power facilities utilizing idle substation sites [104, 112, 115, 116]. Internationally, there are positive signals, such as the United Arab Emirates (UAE) announcing plans to expand renewable energy by 30GW [99], and IRENA (International Renewable Energy Agency) stating that renewable energy combined with batteries is cheaper than coal and gas power generation [100]. However, the uncertainty of renewable energy supply for increasing new power demand is growing, as Microsoft (MS) is re-evaluating its 2030 clean energy target due to the surge in AI data centers [106, 113], and climate and environmental groups are advocating for mandatory renewable energy use in AI data centers [107]. Furthermore, an analysis suggests that domestic market and provincial governors' renewable energy pledges remain at a declarative level, with no decarbonization pledges [95], indicating that more substantive policy measures are required.

sections.title: 📰 Call for Policy and Awareness Improvement for Climate Change Response
sections.content: Policy support and improved public awareness are essential for responding to climate change. Gyeonggi Provincial Assembly member Kim Ho-gyeom emphasized the importance of climate change response and rural support policies, urging institutional backing [4], and Mokpo City is making efforts to protect public health by initiating intensive disinfection to respond to the summer climate crisis [12]. KEPCO demonstrated its commitment to energy transition by appointing carbon neutrality experts to its new outside directors [27], but discussions are needed regarding the profit distribution method for renewable energy generated by offshore wind power [98]. Meanwhile, Boeun County's carbon neutrality basic plan received a low D-grade evaluation [50], highlighting the urgent need for qualitative improvement in the establishment and implementation of local governments' carbon neutrality plans. These developments suggest that climate change response is not merely an environmental issue but requires an integrated approach and sustained attention across all sectors, including economy, society, and governance.

briefing.closing: Today's briefing has highlighted both the severity of the climate crisis and the diverse domestic and international efforts and challenges in overcoming it. We must respond effectively to climate change through continuous attention and action. This concludes today's climate briefing. Thank you.
